golden_categories.name: Basic URL Validation
golden_categories.description: Covers general URL validation with standard protocols and formats.

golden_categories.name: Localhost and Non-TLD URLs
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs without protocols or with localhost and non-TLD domains.

golden_categories.name: Protocol Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests related to handling protocols, including custom protocols and protocol validation.

golden_categories.name: Underscore Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URLs with underscores in the hostname.

golden_categories.name: TLD and Trailing Dot Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests for URLs without TLDs and with trailing dots.

golden_categories.name: Protocol Relative URLs
golden_categories.description: Tests for handling protocol-relative URLs and related options.

golden_categories.name: Fragment and Query Component Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests for handling fragments and query components in URLs.

golden_categories.name: Authentication Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests for handling authentication information in URLs.

golden_categories.name: Host Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating hostnames, including whitelists, blacklists, and regular expressions.

golden_categories.name: Security and Vulnerability Handling
golden_categories.description: Tests for handling security-related cases, such as rejecting invalid protocols and parsing differences.

golden_categories.name: Length Validation
golden_categories.description: Tests for validating URL length and configuring maximum allowed length.

generated_categories.name: Basic URL Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests fundamental URL validation including protocols, host presence, and port range with default options.

generated_categories.name: Protocol and Host Requirements
generated_categories.description: Tests specific requirements for protocol presence and host presence in URLs.

generated_categories.name: Fragment and Query Component Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests the handling of fragments and query components in URLs based on configuration options.

generated_categories.name: Authentication Handling
generated_categories.description: Tests URLs with and without authentication components when authentication is disallowed.

generated_categories.name: Length Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests URL length validation based on maximum allowed length.

generated_categories.name: Custom Protocol Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests URLs against a custom list of allowed protocols.

golden_to_generated.reason: Both categories focus on basic URL validation, including protocols, host presence, and port range.
Both categories address protocol and host requirements, including localhost and non-TLD URLs.
GENC-6 covers custom protocol validation, which overlaps with GC-3's protocol handling but does not fully encompass all aspects of protocol validation.
No generated category addresses underscore handling in URLs.
No generated category addresses TLD and trailing dot handling.
No generated category addresses protocol-relative URLs.
Both categories focus on handling fragments and query components in URLs.
GENC-4 addresses authentication handling but focuses on disallowing authentication, which is only part of GC-8's scope.
No generated category addresses host validation, including whitelists and blacklists.
No generated category addresses security and vulnerability handling.
Both categories focus on URL length validation, including maximum allowed length.

generated_to_golden.reason: GENC-1 aligns with GC-1 in testing fundamental URL validation, including protocols, host presence, and port range.
GENC-2 aligns with GC-2 in addressing protocol and host requirements, including localhost and non-TLD URLs.
GENC-3 aligns with GC-7 in focusing on handling fragments and query components in URLs.
GENC-4 overlaps with GC-8 in authentication handling but focuses specifically on disallowing authentication.
GENC-5 aligns with GC-11 in testing URL length validation, including maximum allowed length.
GENC-6 overlaps with GC-3 in custom protocol validation but does not fully cover all aspects of protocol handling.
